name: Use Case 3: AI-Driven Tables, Figures, and Listings (TFL) Generation
title: CDISC Case 3 - USDM to traceable TFLs (plan -> spec -> ADaM -> ARD -> TFL)
description: Full protocol-to-TFL pipeline. Given a study's uploaded USDM metadata + SDTM datasets, plan the exhaustive, traceable TFL list, audit it, build the ARS-aligned analysis + ADaM specs, derive ADaM from SDTM, generate the ARD (cards/cardx) and rendered TFLs, and assemble an interactive objective->endpoint->SDTM->ADaM->TFL traceability graph. Three human review gates (plan, specs, TFLs) each feed a self-learning skill-refinement loop: reviewer feedback is distilled into durable, per-skill lessons and opened as a PR so the skills improve over time. Artifacts land in /output.

steps.name: Upload inputs
steps.description: Upload everything the run needs, up front: the study's USDM v3+ study-definition JSON AND its SDTM datasets (.xpt, Dataset-JSON, .csv, or .sas7bdat). Nothing is bundled; an upload is required. All uploaded files are made available to the next step (plan-tlfs) read-only under /data/ — plan-tlfs reads the USDM and persists the SDTM datasets into /workspace/sdtm/ so the later ADaM step can use them.

steps.name: Plan required TLFs from USDM
steps.description: AI. The uploaded inputs are read-only under /data/: the USDM study-definition JSON plus the SDTM datasets. STEP 0 (MANDATORY, deterministic — do NOT hand-copy files): run `python3 /app/container/stage_inputs.py`. It content-detects the USDM (by usdmVersion / study.versions), writes it to /workspace/usdm.json, and persists every other upload into /workspace/sdtm/ (the SDTM inventory the later derive-adam step reads); it exits non-zero if no USDM or no SDTM is found — stop and report if so. THEN run the tlf-planner skill on /workspace/usdm.json: interpret objectives/endpoints, add ICH E3 regulatory scaffolding, fan out statistical analyses (imputation/subgroup/timepoint), check data feasibility against the staged SDTM domains, and number the outputs. Writes study-model.json, tlf-plan.json, and the human-readable tlf-index.md to both /workspace and /output, and a /output/result.json summary. On a revise re-entry, read the reviewer comment from the review-plan step and append it to /workspace/review_feedback.jsonl as {"skill":"tlf-planner","iteration":N,"comment":"..."}, then correct only the flagged items.

steps.name: Audit TLF plan (traceability + completeness)
steps.description: AI. Run the tlf-plan-critic skill: independently audit /workspace/tlf-plan.json against /workspace/study-model.json (fresh context, reads only the plan + study model). Two-way coverage: every objective and resolved endpoint maps to >=1 planned TLF; every TLF traces to an endpoint/objective or a regulatory rule. Writes coverage-report.md to /workspace and /output, plus /output/result.json with the verdict.

steps.name: Review TLF plan
steps.description: Review the planned TLF list (/output/tlf-index.md) and the independent audit (/output/coverage-report.md). Approve to build the analysis + ADaM specs, or request changes to send the run back to Plan required TLFs with your comment (the planner captures it for the skill-refinement loop).

params.name: comment
params.description: What to change about the TLF plan (required when requesting changes).

steps.name: Build analysis + ADaM specs
steps.description: AI. Run the tlf-analysis-spec skill on the approved plan: for each producible TLF emit an ARS-aligned analysis-spec entry (analysisSet, groupingFactor, dataSubset, method + operations), and aggregate the variable-level adam-spec.json (datasets, PARAMCDs, flags) INCLUDING the 3 mandatory derivation rules + the data-quality N-gate. Writes analysis-spec.json + adam-spec.json to /workspace and /output, plus /output/result.json. On a revise re-entry, append the reviewer comment to /workspace/review_feedback.jsonl as {"skill":"tlf-analysis-spec",...} and fix only the flagged specs.

steps.name: Review analysis + ADaM specs
steps.description: Review the analysis specs (methods, populations, contrasts) and the ADaM spec (datasets, flags, mandatory derivation rules) in /output. Approve to derive ADaM, or request changes to send the run back to Build specs with your comment.

params.name: comment
params.description: What to change about the specs (required when requesting changes).

steps.name: Derive ADaM from SDTM
steps.description: AI. Run the sdtm-to-adam skill: derive the BDS/OCCDS/TTE ADaM datasets that /workspace/adam-spec.json demands from the SDTM datasets at /workspace/sdtm/ (persisted from the upload by plan-tlfs) using admiral. ENFORCE the 3 mandatory rules (day-based visit windowing that keeps unscheduled/ET visits; LOCF endpoint records; SITEGR1 pooling on randomized counts) and the data-quality N-gate (assert each analysis-set N against its population flag, fail on shortfall). Writes /workspace/adam/*.csv (+ derivation code + a conformance report) and /output/result.json.

steps.name: Generate ARD + TLFs
steps.description: AI. Run the tlf-generator skill: for each TLF, compute the ARD (numbers) from ADaM per the analysis-spec using cards/cardx + emmeans directly (explicit contrast signs; MMRM via mmrm + custom ARD), then render the display (gtsummary tables, ggsurvfit figures) applying SAS rounding at display precision. Keep numbers and formatting as separate phases. Writes /workspace/{ard,tfl,code}/<id>.* and /output/ (rendered TLFs, ard) + /output/result.json. On a revise re-entry, append the reviewer comment to /workspace/review_feedback.jsonl as {"skill":"tlf-generator",...} and fix only the flagged outputs.

steps.name: Review generated TLFs
steps.description: Review the rendered TLFs in /output. Approve to assemble traceability, or request changes to send the run back to Generate ARD + TLFs with your comment.

params.name: comment
params.description: What to change about the generated TLFs (required when requesting changes).

steps.name: Assemble traceability graph (deterministic)
steps.description: Deterministic. Join study-model.json, tlf-plan.json, analysis-spec.json, adam-spec.json, the staged SDTM inventory (/workspace/sdtm/), and the per-TLF ARD/rendered/code artifacts (/workspace/{ard,tfl,code}/) into the objective->endpoint->SDTM->ADaM->TLF graph model (nodes, edges, two-way coverage, issues feed, counts, status tallies) per the traceability-builder graph-data-schema. No LLM — the correctness-critical join that a language model must not guess. Writes /workspace/trace_graph.json + /workspace/manifest.json (and copies both to /output), plus /output/result.json.

steps.name: Render traceability explorer
steps.description: AI. RENDER ONLY — the graph is already assembled deterministically by assemble-trace-graph; do NOT recompute coverage, edges, status, or the issues feed. Run the traceability-builder skill in render mode: read /workspace/trace_graph.json (the authoritative node/edge/issues model) and emit a single self-contained interactive node-graph HTML explorer (vanilla JS + SVG, zero external requests) with lineage highlighting, per-node detail panels (rendered TLF + status), an issues panel, type/status filters, search, and light/dark theming. Writes /output/traceability.html and copies /output/trace_graph.json + /output/manifest.json, plus /output/result.json.

steps.name: Check for review feedback
steps.description: Deterministic gate. Read /workspace/review_feedback.jsonl (reviewer comments captured by plan-tlfs, build-specs, and generate-tlfs on each revise) and emit { hadRevisions, revisionCount, skills } to /output/result.json. Routes the run into the skill-refinement tail (propose-skill-update -> open-skill-pr) only when a review actually sent something back; a clean first-pass run skips both and goes straight to done.

steps.name: Propose skill lessons from review feedback
steps.description: AI. Self-learning loop, generalized across all three review gates. Read /workspace/review_feedback.jsonl (reviewer comments captured by plan-tlfs, build-specs, and generate-tlfs on each revise, each tagged with its skill), the approved artifacts, and each target skill's current references/lessons-learned.md. For EACH skill that was revised, distil durable, skill-general lessons and emit a per-skill append block. Writes /output/result.json as { hasLessons, lessons: [ { skill, lessonAppendMarkdown } ], prTitle, prBody, summary }. When review_feedback.jsonl is absent/empty (first-pass approvals) emit hasLessons=false and no lessons.

steps.name: Open skill-lesson PR
steps.description: Deterministic. If propose-skill-update produced lessons (hasLessons=true), fresh-clone SKILL_REPO with GITHUB_TOKEN, append each lesson's block to that skill's plugins/cdisc-case-3/skills/<skill>/references/lessons-learned.md, branch skill-lesson/<runId>, commit as Mediforce Bot, push, and open one PR against main covering all touched skills. hasLessons=false (or byte-identical files) => no PR, clean exit. Idempotent on retry: force-pushes the branch and reuses an existing open PR.
